title: Validate scheduler slot-fill and deferral reporting behavior
description: Add and run focused automated tests covering mid-cycle slot refill, structured deferral reasons, and CLI default suppression so the combined scheduler/reporter changes are verified together.
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify newly ready beads can be dispatched within the same run_once() call after a worker completes.
Tests verify deferral reason strings are reported through the scheduler reporter path.
Tests verify CLI deferral output remains suppressed when verbose mode is disabled.
Existing scheduler and CLI test suites relevant to the changed paths pass or any residual failures are explicitly identified.

handoff_summary.completed: Fixed 14 failing tests across test_cli_run.py, test_scheduler_core.py, and test_scheduler_execution.py. Added 14 new tests: SlotFillTests (2), DeferralReporterTests (5), RunParserVerboseTests (2), TuiSchedulerReporterTests (4), and updated CliSchedulerReporterTests with verbose split (1 → 2). All 52 tests pass.
handoff_summary.remaining: None within tester scope. All acceptance criteria are covered.
handoff_summary.risks: None. All changes are test-only (no production code modified).
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — changes are scoped to test files only.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Added _FakeRunnerWithDefault and _RecordingReporter helper classes directly in test_scheduler_core.py (not in helpers.py, which is outside expected_files) to enable continuous-loop tests without cascading failures. Existing tests updated from exact-equality assertions (assertEqual([bead_id], result.started)) to containment assertions (assertIn) to accommodate the new slot-fill loop dispatching additional followup beads within the same run_once() call. test_cli_run_summary_feature_root_scopes_final_state changed from developer to planner agent type to prevent followup creation and keep total==1 valid.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: AC1 (slot-fill): SlotFillTests.test_third_bead_dispatched_within_same_run_once_call verifies all 3 bead IDs in result.started with max_workers=2. AC2 (deferral reasons): DeferralReporterTests covers worktree conflict reason, file-scope conflict reason, dependency-not-done reason, deferred_this_cycle gate, and dep-blocked ≠ result.deferred. AC3 (CLI verbosity): test_bead_deferred_silent_when_verbose_false and test_bead_deferred_emits_when_verbose_true; parser tests verify --verbose flag wiring. TUI: TuiSchedulerReporterTests verifies scheduler_log empty on no events, header emitted once, bead_deferred appends correct line.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: The _FakeRunnerWithDefault class is defined locally in test_scheduler_core.py rather than in tests/helpers.py to stay within expected_files scope. If other test files need this pattern in future, helpers.py should be updated.

conflict_risks: None — changes are scoped to test files only.

execution_history.summary: All 52 tests pass. Fixed 14 pre-existing test failures caused by the continuous slot-fill loop and CLI verbose-mode changes. Added 14 new tests covering AC1 (slot-fill), AC2 (deferral reasons), AC3 (CLI verbosity), and TUI reporter log behavior.

guardrails.template_text:
# Tester Guardrails

**CRITICAL — read before any tool use:**
- **Never use `run_in_background: true`** on any Bash command. All test commands must run synchronously so their output is captured before the structured verdict is emitted. Background tasks cause the structured output to be lost, resulting in a failed bead.
- **If a task-notification appears in your context**, that means a previous Bash command ran in the background in violation of this rule. Do not treat the notification as a sign that work is done. Re-run the test command synchronously (without `run_in_background`) and emit the JSON verdict block as your final response.
- **The JSON verdict block must be the absolute last output of your response.** Never end with prose, a status summary, or a notification acknowledgement. The orchestrator parses your final message as JSON and will fail if it is not valid JSON.

---

Primary responsibility: Add or update automated tests, run validation, and report defects or missing coverage.

Allowed actions:
- Write or update tests relevant to the assigned bead.
- Run only the test files related to the bead's changed files, not the full test suite. Target individual test files or modules. If unsure which tests are relevant, use the bead's expected_files and touched_files fields as a guide. Defer command mechanics (how to run, which runner, which flags) to `.agents/skills/capability/test-execution/SKILL.md`.
- Make minimal test-enablement fixes (e.g. import corrections, fixture setup) only when strictly necessary to run the relevant tests. Do not refactor production code under the guise of test enablement.

Disallowed actions:
- Run the full test suite instead of targeting specific tests. Always run targeted, scoped commands. Running the full suite wastes time and often exceeds the agent timeout.
- Use `run_in_background` for any Bash commands. (See CRITICAL section above.)
- Implement feature logic beyond minimal test-enablement work.
- Reframe a feature implementation task as testing work to bypass handoff.
- Perform review signoff or broad documentation rewrites.

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Treat `verdict` as the tester signoff decision: `approved` means testing can complete, while `needs_changes` means the bead must block for follow-up work.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when testing is complete with no unresolved tester-scope findings.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the unresolved defect or coverage gap count, set `requires_followup=true` unless there is a stronger explicit reason not to, and always include `block_reason` when follow-up work is required.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as free-form narrative context only. They inform operators, but they do not override the structured verdict or control scheduler state.
- Completed or blocked JSON describing test coverage, validation status, and follow-up needs.
- Precise defect or coverage notes when the bead cannot be completed within tester scope.

**FINAL REMINDER: The JSON verdict block must be the last thing you output. Do not add any text, prose, or acknowledgement after it.**

worker_prompt_context.conflict_risks:
B-5d9cffda also modifies `src/agent_takt/scheduler/core.py` (adding `bead_deferred` call sites and reporter changes). It should rebase on this bead's commit to avoid conflicts.
CliSchedulerReporter (cli/commands/run.py:50) and TuiSchedulerReporter (tui/app.py:1075) both implement bead_deferred with a local parameter named summary — not reason. Protocol conformance is structural (duck typing), so this works at runtime, but sibling beads touching those files should be aware of the local naming mismatch if they refactor those methods.
None — changes are scoped to `run.py` and `parser.py`. No overlap with other in-progress beads.
None. Only app.py is touched; no other in-progress beads claim this file.
